new_terms.context: NVLink 6 doubles bandwidth by implementing bi-directional signaling over the same number of copper cables.
Using a simultaneous bi-directional SerDes for the copper backplane instead of increasing the modulation.
An inverted copy of the local TX must be generated at the local RX for proper echo cancellation.
Each electrical lane is one differential pair (DP) consisting of two conductors.
The evolution of scale-up and scale-out networking speeds.
The scale-up network continues to be rail-optimized with all-to-all connectivity.
A module that acts as a host to the switch tray and manages system functions.
Bandwidth used for internal rack communication, typically via NVLink.
Bandwidth used for inter-node or cluster-level communication, typically via InfiniBand or Ethernet.
A signaling method used in NVLink 6 that requires higher PCB material quality due to lower insertion loss tolerance.
Passive copper connections used to link GPU trays and switch trays in the NVL72 rack.
Refers to the number of data paths per lane or connector in the NVLink switch architecture.
A specific grade of low dielectric constant glass fiber cloth used in high-speed PCB manufacturing.
Active Electrical Cables used for NIC-to-TOR and switch-to-switch connections.
A networking architecture that splits traffic across multiple independent switch fabrics.
A networking topology where each GPU has a dedicated path (rail) to the network.
Specialized cloud providers focused on AI and GPU infrastructure, distinct from hyperscalers.
The scale-out network used for GPU-to-GPU communication in a cluster.
Traditional transceiver modules as opposed to Co-Packaged Optics (CPO).
NVIDIA's next-generation InfiniBand switch series.
A hardware feature in SN6800 switches to manage internal fiber routing for multi-plane architectures.
A condition where a network interface on a router/switch goes up and down repeatedly.
A component used in co-packaged optics (CPO) to provide the laser for optical engines.
A network design where traffic is split across multiple independent switch planes to increase scale.
NVIDIA's brand for its interconnect products, including cables and transceivers.
The total throughput capacity of a switch or network system.
Physical fiber management used to fan out optical signals to different logical ports or switch boxes.
Multi-headed cables used to connect a single high-bandwidth port to multiple lower-bandwidth ports.
A type of network fabric architecture used by Meta, typically based on Ethernet rather than scheduled protocols like InfiniBand.
High-speed copper cables with active circuitry to improve signal integrity, used for NIC-to-TOR connections.
A component used to convert between different SerDes speeds, such as 200G per lane to 100G per lane.
The switch located at the top of a server rack connecting the nodes within the rack to the rest of the network.
